title: Architecture uses three evidence layers
source_observation: Textual descriptions archaeological remains and production reconstruction do not carry equal certainty
risk: A single blended design may present interpretation as excavated fact
decision: Label textual archaeological and cinematic architecture separately in every major set dossier
rationale: Preserves traceability while allowing coherent sets
required_review: Classical Tamil; archaeology; architecture; production design

title: No universal ring-well function
source_observation: Ring wells occur in varied contexts and their purpose depends on stratigraphy and associated features
risk: The film may label every ring well a sewer or drinking well
decision: Assign a function only after site-specific review and preserve uncertainty in publicity
rationale: Prevents familiar but unsupported infrastructure claims

title: Puhar remains a distributed water-port city
source_observation: Literary and archaeological evidence support a river-sea city with wharf and diverse civic functions
risk: A monumental stone harbour and rigid grid would impose later or foreign forms
decision: Build linked landing storage work residential ritual and public-care zones shaped by water movement
rationale: Integrates harbour architecture with labour and social life

title: Prison conversion changes operations
source_observation: The epic changes a prison into a place of lodging kitchens and feeding
risk: A cosmetic redress would miss the political transformation
decision: Redesign circulation access water storage kitchens supervision and social roles when the institution changes
rationale: Makes reform visible as public policy
required_review: Legal history; architecture; food service; screenplay

title: Chakravala is not horror fantasy
source_observation: The cremation-ground boundary landscape carries death refuge and philosophical meaning
risk: Gothic spectacle could erase its ethical function
decision: Use material traces of death and ritual restraint without monsters or generic haunted-cemetery design
required_review: Classical Tamil; funerary archaeology; religious history; director

title: Later monuments cannot stand in for early Kanchi
source_observation: Kanchi's surviving monumental identity is dominated by later periods
risk: Using later Pallava or Chola forms would collapse chronology
decision: Develop early historic Kanchi from settlement water trade and institutional evidence and keep later monuments outside the default visual language
required_review: Kanchi archaeology; architectural history; production design
